author: JackMediaComments
title: Everyone Hates Printers, and it’s HP’s fault
content: Printers have always been hated, but they used to have more hardware issues than software. Every time my mom needs to print or scan something this software is the issue. Required sign in is obviously a dark practice to sell user data, and it ruins the efficiency of the workflow. Then after complying to that privacy invasion, I am told “printer not responding” “scanner unavailable” and the solution is to restart? Anyone involved in planning, coding, and/or promoting this app should go on strike, and then just quit, for the betterment of society. If you rated this app above 2 stars, please include how much they are paying you. I’m so relieved that my generation never uses scanners or printers, I am excited for this company to go bankrupt and die. Shout out to the movie Office Space, I hate everything about printers and it’s their fault.

author: Bleepless in Bellingham
title: Genuine HP ink cartridge not good enough for HP
content:
Bought a genuine HP ink cartridge, I used it to print a couple pages, and HP suddenly bricked it for being “HP Protected on another printer.” Clearly some sort of malfunction, but a pretty ridiculous “protection” feature, HP. And now I’ve lost half an hour troubleshooting this nonsense.

So, let’s get this straight—if I had purchased HP ink and my printer broke, and I bought another HP printer to use that same ink cartridge in (because the ink cartridges cost almost as much as a new printer, and why would I want to trash a brand new ink cartridge?), HP would refuse to use that cartridge because another HP printer had used it? That doesn’t “protect” anything but HP’s profits. 

Fellow consumers, when you think corporate greed, think HP. Gross way to treat your customers. Wasteful way to treat your product. Is it worth degrading yourself like this so you could pay your CEO $23 million dollars last year?

author: amjice
title: iOS App (Updated)
content:
App keeps getting better and better: the ability to select a 2-sided scan is great, thank you!

While the laptop version of the app is more robust, and understandably so,at I request that a 600 DPI scan control option be made available from within the app?

Firstly, the iOS app is far more convenient to control the scanner than it is to boot up the Windows laptop and use the more robust control interface.

Secondly, more and more travelers and security professionals recommend saving a digital copy in a folder structure in iOS and 600 DPI seems to be the norm for important documents like passports, driver licenses, birth certificates, etc. to capture the fine security details and other official imprints. 

It would only make sense, then, to use the iOS app and save directly into the iPhone / iPad than to separately boot up a laptop and then have to transfer it to the smartphone.

Thanks for your consideration of this important, often overlooked feature!

OLD: Would be great if the iOS Smart app can scan _both_sides_ from the document feeder like it already can using the Windows version of the app.  I can't imagine that would be a difficult feature to make available.

author: Laurin Dugas(Lafayette,LA.)
title: Beautiful & Super Easy
content: Really easy from the app printing and photos come out stunning. It’s hard to believe these results come from a relatively inexpensive but quality home printer. Very satisfied with both the printer and the results.

title: HP envy 5055
content: Greatly equipped with fax, scans, and you can email your files. I love it.

author: Vicky gentry
title: Tinta se agota rápido
content: No es posible que solo pude imprimir 101 páginas en BORRADOR de color negro y ya el cartucho se acabó, cuando supuestamente dura de 600 a 700 páginas en impresión normal y yo imprimí en borrador, no es justo.

author: tanpham10
title: HP Printers and Instant Ink connectivity is the Future.
content: I have always used brothers printers for the past many years they reliable dependable I can find alternative inks for less but it’s time for me to upgrade and I came across this HP printer when it went on sale at Best Buy.  As I set it up I came across the instant Ink services it will deliver the ink when my printer runs low on ink for free shipping so I don’t have to worry about shopping for ink.  Also I know I will always be using the Genuine ink cartridge from HP.  i’m very impressed with this new service I just hope the printer itself will last a long time.

author: untakennickname337
title: setup is insane…
content:
love the hardware but the configuration sw and documentation is unusable.  go to one of 3 built-in web servers.  email server is built in and/or remote.  written docs are misleading and often incorrect.

of all the features, i can print from my ipad, but scanning, faxing is complex and just doesn’t work.

i’ve used hp solely for 20+ years, but is going back and buying a different brand.  no more hp in my future.

author: EW32
title: How to take a great product and destroy it!!!
content: From the HP Smart Printing app, I could print and document, email, or webpage from my phone.  With this new app, the document appears to print but doesn’t. Also had a difficult time finding my printers (one only months old) and had to use the serial number to identify.  And docs still don’t print. So this basically took print functionality away from my phone.
